mnemonics.title: Block Unblock Set
mnemonics.content: Remember the three 'how' parameters: B-US (Block-Unblock-Set): B = Block (add to mask), U = Unblock (remove from mask), S = Set (replace entire mask)

mnemonics.title: sigprocmask Parameters Order
mnemonics.content: Think of 'HOW-SET-OLD': sigprocmask(HOW, SET, OLD) - Alphabetical order of operations: how to apply, what to set, where to store old

acronyms.expansion: SIGnal PROCess MASK - manipulate process signal mask
acronyms.usage: Used to block/unblock signals for process protection

acronyms.expansion: SIGnal SET TYPE - data type representing a set of signals
acronyms.usage: Used as parameter to sigprocmask to specify which signals to block

acronyms.expansion: SIGnal BLOCK - add signals to current mask
acronyms.usage: Used in sigprocmask to add specified signals to existing blocked set

acronyms.expansion: SIGnal UNBLOCK - remove signals from current mask
acronyms.usage: Used in sigprocmask to remove signals from blocked set

acronyms.expansion: SIGnal SET MASK - replace entire signal mask
acronyms.usage: Used in sigprocmask to completely replace existing mask with new set

visualTips.visualization: Imagine signal mask as a curtain blocking signals (like water). Blocked signals cannot pass through to the handler. When you unblock, the 'curtain' opens and signals flow through to trigger handlers.
Think of pending signals as people waiting at a door (blocked entrance). When the door opens (unmasked), they all enter immediately. The handler is like the reception desk they go to.
Use Venn diagram: SIG_BLOCK = Union (add to existing), SIG_UNBLOCK = Difference (remove from existing), SIG_SETMASK = Replacement (discard old, use new set entirely)

analogies.analogy: Like putting your phone on 'Do Not Disturb' mode - calls (signals) come in but are blocked/not delivered until you turn off DND.
Like email in spam folder - emails arrive but are held until you mark them as 'not spam' (unblock), then they appear in inbox (handler invoked).
SIG_BLOCK is like adding more items to your shopping list (OR), while SIG_SETMASK is like throwing away old list and writing completely new one (replace).
